what: Dim(dim = 1, type = NULL, lower = NULL, upper = NULL, random = FALSE)
title: introduction
text: Specifies dimensions, type, bounds, random effects parameter. dim: Parameter dimensions (e.g., scalar=1, vector=length, matrix=c(rows, columns)). type: Parameter type (e.g., "vector", "matrix", "ordered", "simplex", "corr_matrix", "cov_matrix", "CF_corr"). omitted, automatically inferred dimensions. lower: Lower bound parameter. upper: Upper bound parameter. random: TRUE, treats parameter random effect, making subject Laplace approximation (applicable MAP estimation MCMC).

what: rtmb_model(data, code, par_names = list(), init = NULL, view = NULL, null_target = NULL)
title: introduction
text: Combines data model definition blocks build R6 class model object. data: Named list containing observation data constants. code: Model definition block generated rtmb_code(). par_names: List specific names corresponding dimensions parameter (optional). init: List numeric vector initial values. omitted, values automatically generated randomly. view: Character vector parameter names display preferentially summary() outputs. null_target: Used construct null model simultaneously; specify target parameter fix prior distribution disable (e.g., "delta ~ cauchy(0, r)").

previous_headings: BayesRTMB Package Reference Manual > 2. Estimation Methods (Methods of RTMB_Model)
title: introduction
text: Performs Maximum Posteriori (MAP) estimation Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE). * Arguments: * num_estimate: Number times run estimation avoid local optima. * laplace: TRUE, marginalizes random effects using Laplace approximation. * optimizer: Specifies optimizer ("nlminb" "optim"). * method: Optimization method using optim (e.g., "BFGS"). * map: Used fix parameters. Generally manipulated automatically via $null_model().

previous_headings: BayesRTMB Package Reference Manual > 2. Estimation Methods (Methods of RTMB_Model)
title: introduction
text: Draws posterior samples using -U-Turn Sampler (NUTS). * Arguments: * sampling: Number sampling iterations per chain. * warmup: Number warmup (burn-) iterations per chain. * chains: Number Markov chains. * thin: Thinning interval. * delta: Target acceptance rate NUTS (default: 0.8). * max_treedepth: Maximum tree depth NUTS (default: 10). * parallel: TRUE, runs chains parallel using future package. * laplace: TRUE, performs NUTS sampling marginalized posterior using Laplace approximation.

previous_headings: BayesRTMB Package Reference Manual > 2. Estimation Methods (Methods of RTMB_Model)
title: introduction
text: Performs Automatic Differentiation Variational Inference (ADVI). * Arguments: * iter: Number optimization iterations. * tol_rel_obj: Convergence threshold (relative change ELBO). * num_samples: Number posterior samples generate fitted variational distribution. * num_estimate: Number times run ADVI (treated chains avoid local optima). * alpha: Learning rate optimizer. * laplace: TRUE, applies ADVI marginalized posterior using Laplace approximation.

title: introduction
text: Stores results $optimize(). * Fields: par (parameter list constrained scale), objective (negative log-posterior), log_ml (log marginal likelihood via Laplace approximation), df_fixed / random_effects (summary data frames estimates). * Methods: * $summary(max_rows = 20, digits = 2): Outputs table summarizing estimates, standard errors, 95% Wald confidence intervals.

previous_headings: BayesRTMB Package Reference Manual > 4. Fit Result Objects
title: introduction
text: Stores results $sample() performs post-processing posterior samples. * Methods: * $summary(pars = NULL, ...): Returns data frame summary statistics including Rhat, effective sample size (ESS), posterior mean, credible intervals. * $draws(pars = NULL, chains = NULL, inc_random = FALSE, inc_transform = TRUE, inc_generate = TRUE): Extracts posterior samples 3D array (iteration, chain, variable). * $bridgesampling(method = "warp3", seed = NULL): Estimates marginal likelihood via Bridge Sampling. * $generated_quantities(code): Computes generated quantities (GQ) extracted posterior samples stores object.

previous_headings: BayesRTMB Package Reference Manual > 4. Fit Result Objects
title: introduction
text: Stores results $variational(). * Methods: * $summary(pars = NULL, ...): Returns data frame summary statistics (Note: Rhat ESS computed). * $draws(pars = NULL, best_only = TRUE, ...): Extracts posterior samples. best_only = TRUE, extracts samples run highest ELBO. * $plot_elbo(tail_n = 1000): Plots Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO) history optimization. * $plot_trajectory(pars = NULL): Plots parameter trajectory final optimization window. * $generated_quantities(code): Computes generated quantities.

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: BayesRTMB R package fast, flexible, compile-free Bayesian modeling. Powered RTMB (R template Model Builder) automatic differentiation engine, BayesRTMB allows write models natively R syntax similar Stan, eliminating need lengthy C++ compilation times.

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: C++ Compilation: Write models pure R execute instantly. Intuitive Block Syntax: Define model cleanly using rtmb_code() parameters, transform, model, generate blocks. NUTS (-U-Turn Sampler) robust MCMC sampling. ADVI (Automatic Differentiation Variational Inference) fast approximate inference. MAP (Maximum Posteriori) estimation. Laplace Approximation: Built-support marginalizing random effects speed complex hierarchical models. Model Comparison: Compute marginal likelihoods via Bridge Sampling calculate Bayes Factors. Rich Diagnostics & Plotting: Integrated functions trace plots, density plots, autocorrelation, forest plots. Convenient Wrappers: Ready--use functions like rtmb_ttest rtmb_glmer standard analyses automatic weakly informative priors.

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: can install development version GitHub :

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: model BayesRTMB defined combining data rtmb_code() block. simple example estimating mean standard deviation.
code: library(BayesRTMB)  # 1. Prepare data Y <- c(5.2, 4.8, 5.5, 6.1, 4.9, 5.3) dat <- list(Y = Y, N = length(Y))  # 2. Define the model using rtmb_code code <- rtmb_code(   parameters = {     mu = Dim(1)     sigma = Dim(1, lower = 0)   },   model = {     # Priors     mu ~ normal(0, 10)     sigma ~ exponential(0.1)          # Likelihood     Y ~ normal(mu, sigma)   } )  # 3. Build the model object mdl <- rtmb_model(data = dat, code = code)  # 4. Run NUTS MCMC sampling fit_mcmc <- mdl$sample(iter = 2000, warmup = 1000, chains = 4)  # Print summary fit_mcmc$summary()

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: BayesRTMB provides unified interface various inference methods using R6 objects. can easily apply different algorithms exact model object.
code: # Maximum a Posteriori (MAP) estimation fit_map <- mdl$optimize()  # Automatic Differentiation Variational Inference (ADVI) # Supports "meanfield", "fullrank", and "hybrid" approaches fit_advi <- mdl$advi(method = "meanfield", iter = 5000)

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: hierarchical models, can declare parameters random effects. BayesRTMB can utilize RTMB’s Laplace approximation integrate , significantly speeding MAP NUTS.
code: code_hierarchical <- rtmb_code(   parameters = {     mu_global = Dim(1)     sigma_global = Dim(1, lower = 0)     # Declare a random effect vector     alpha = Dim(J, random = TRUE)    },   model = {     alpha ~ normal(mu_global, sigma_global)     # ... rest of the model ...   } )  # Enable Laplace approximation during sampling fit_mcmc_laplace <- mdl$sample(laplace = TRUE)

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: BayesRTMB integrates bridge sampling easily compute marginal likelihoods model comparison. can evaluate strength evidence one model another using Bayes factors.
code: # Assume fit_model1 and fit_model2 are MCMC_Fit objects # Compute marginal likelihoods via Bridge Sampling bs1 <- fit_model1$bridgesampling() bs2 <- fit_model2$bridgesampling()  # Calculate Bayes Factor # This function outputs the BF and an interpretation based on Kass & Raftery (1995) bayes_factor(bs1, bs2)

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: package includes built-functions visualize posterior distributions diagnose MCMC performance quickly.
code: # Extract the underlying array of samples samples <- fit_mcmc$fit  # Diagnostic plots plot_trace(samples) plot_dens(samples) plot_acf(samples, var_idx = 1)  # Compare parameter estimates plot_forest(samples, prob = 0.95) plot_pairs(samples)

title: Bayesian Inference using RTMB
text: don’t want write model code scratch, BayesRTMB offers wrapper functions common tasks, complete automated weakly informative priors.
code: # Bayesian Two-Sample T-Test # Automatically scales priors based on y_range mdl_test <- rtmb_ttest(   y1 = group_A,    y2 = group_B,    y_range = c(0,10) )  fit_test <- mdl_test$sample()

text: result evaluating expr. error occurs, throws formatted [stop()] message.

title: Catch and Translate RTMB Errors into User-Friendly Messages — with_rtmb_error_handling
text: uses regular expressions categorize common RTMB failure modes, : **Missing Objects/Functions**: Detects variable specific distribution   function (e.g., `normal_lpdf`) missing environment. **Index Errors**: Identifies subscript bounds errors. **AD Type Destruction**: Catches "lost class attribute" error, often caused   incorrect initialization vectors (e.g., using `numeric(N)` partial assignment   inside loops). **Matrix Dimension Mismatch**: Identifies non-conformable arguments   matrix multiplication.
